en: All right. It's versatile. Good.
Thank you very much. Inception.
Now, before you bother telling me it's impossible...
No, it's perfectly possible.
It's just bloody difficult. Interesting.
Arthur keeps telling me it can't be done.
Hmm. Arthur. Are you still working with that stick-in-the-mud?
ko: 아서. 아직도 그 고집쟁이랑 일하고 있어?

en: He is good at what he does, right?
Oh, he's the best. He has no imagination. Not like you.
ko: 오, 그는 최고야. 상상력이 없어. 너처럼은 아니지.

en: Listen, if you're going to perform Inception, you need imagination.
Let me ask you something.
Have you done it before? We tried it.
We got the idea in place, but it didn't take.
You didn't plant it deep enough?
No, it's not just about depth.
You need the simplest version of the idea in order for it to grow naturally in your subject's mind.
ko: 아이디어의 가장 단순한 버전이 필요해, 그래야 자연스럽게 대상의 마음에 자랄 수 있어.

en: It's a very subtle art.
So what is this idea that you need to plan?
We need the heir of a major corporation to dissolve his father's empire.
ko: 우리는 대기업의 상속자가 아버지의 제국을 해체해야 해.

en: You see right there, you have various political motivations and anti-monopolistic sentiments and so forth.
ko: 바로 그 점에서 다양한 정치적 동기와 반독점 감정 등이 있어.

en: And all of that stuff.
It's really at the mercy of your subject's prejudice, you see.
ko: 정말로 대상의 편견에 좌우되는 거야, 알겠지.
